text:
There once was a very small frog who lived on a little leaf pond near a tiny stream. Every day she'd watch butterflies float lazily down the stream.

One sunny afternoon, a beautiful swallowtail butterfly landed on her head. The frog felt proud.

She'd never felt such happiness! But alas, gravity wasn't a frog friend. Off she went tumbling down the stream instead.

But the swallowtail never fell far. She landed gracefully on a large autumn leaf. "I think I'm stuck!"

With gentle encouragement from a nearby songbird, she fluttered onto an eagle feather.

The eagle soared. The swallowtail saw stars.

Suddenly snapping awake, thanks to a forest breeze, she soared skyward once more.

With newfound wingspan, she flew into the sun, leaving behind a tiny feather.

Far downstream, an otter saw the feather.

"Where did I come from?" the otter wondered.

"Ah," he thought, "a friend." With delight he hid it
